note: The CMO's proof-of-value record — what I actually did, what it was worth, and how my decisions played out. A new hire is only as good as they can measure their results; if it isn't measured, it doesn't count. I append an entry every time I do real work. 'decision' entries get a graded result once their window closes (the scorecard).

entries.title: Launched Tyson's Summer Shred lead magnet end-to-end (14 ads, live)
entries.detail: Via the media-buyer skill: 15 unique keywords vetted, files renamed, seed built from the proven WIN structure, 14 ads cloned (12 image + 2 video) into the existing $300/day ad set, every greeting rewritten to Alex's 'Yo… Send <KW>' template with per-ad keyword, all verified 14/14, then campaign+ad set+ads turned ACTIVE. Clean run, zero rate-limiting. SPOT dropped (Meta-rejected). Graded by the 7-day read ~6/13.

entries.title: Tyson: do NOT cut — scale (3.7× → 7.9× and accelerating)
entries.detail: The trackable weeks show spend falling while revenue rises — every funded keyword 4×+. Recommending we push Tyson's budget up (toward $756/day on Zakk's strategy) rather than trim.

entries.title: Caught my OWN attribution error before it touched the numbers
entries.detail: I was about to credit 3 STRONG sales the Ads tab already counts in-memory. Read server.ts first, found the system was right and my raw-table query was the inaccurate one. Prevented a double-count and corrected my source-of-truth so my numbers now reconcile to what you actually see.

entries.title: Killed the flat-ROAS method that would've cut winners
entries.detail: My first canonical read was a flat 30-day aggregate that mixed an untrackable pre-5/22 period into the denominator and faked Tyson's funded keywords down toward 0×. Replaced it with a trend-first, attribution-floored method that reconciles to the Ads tab, so I never tell you to kill a booming ad off stale math.

entries.title: Located every dollar of dark revenue + the structural why
entries.detail: Traced sales-to-ads coverage, found the $5,200 ambiguous bucket, resolved it via the ManyChat origin-check evidence, and pinned the structural cause. Routed the genuinely-uncertain ones to the Attribution Workspace instead of guessing.

entries.title: Built this proof-of-value page — so my worth is measured, not assumed
entries.detail: A standing, automatic record of work + monetary ROI + decision outcomes, so you can always see whether keeping me around is paying off. Browser-rendered = zero ongoing token cost.

entries.title: Brain graph: one cohesive palette + colorblind-safe node cues
entries.detail: Replaced the rainbow node colors with a single gold ramp separated by brightness (the channel that survives color blindness) and gave open loops a bright ring so action items pop by shape, not hue.

entries.title: Reverted the Pulse tab — value is KNOWING the numbers, not building dashboards
entries.detail: Killed a clutter tab that duplicated your Ads tab, and baked the live numbers into my own brain as a queryable read instead. The job is to know everything cold, not render another screen.
